title: Peloton Earnings Beat Expectations As It Still Loses Subscribers
text: Peloton Q3 2024 Earnings Beat with Subscriber Challenges. Revenue Beat: Peloton reported revenue that exceeded expectations, beating analyst forecasts on the top line. Earnings Miss: Despite beating on revenue, the company missed on earnings per share, indicating operational challenges in converting revenue to profits. Subscriber Trends: The company continues to lose subscribers overall, with connected fitness subscription declines of 7.6% year-over-year, though at a slower rate than previous quarters. Churn Improvement: Average net monthly churn improved to 1.2%, showing the company is successfully retaining existing subscribers despite pricing increases. Leadership Changes: CEO Barry McCarthy was replaced during this period, with interim co-CEOs managing operations while the company searches for a permanent leader. Restructuring Impact: The restructuring to cut $200 million in annual expenses was announced, signaling the company's commitment to profitability over growth. CEO Search: The board's search for a new permanent CEO suggests the company is looking for a leader to drive the next phase of growth and profitability.
